pattern: Free swaps inside either array mean only the two multisets matter, never positions -- so this reduces to comparing per-value counts: an odd combined count for any value makes the goal unreachable, and otherwise every unit of imbalance between the two arrays is fixed two values at a time, two swaps at a time.
hints: Since same-array swaps are free, positions are irrelevant -- only how many times each value occurs in each array matters.
For every distinct value, if the total number of its occurrences across both arrays is odd, the arrays can never be made identical -- return -1 immediately.

Otherwise sum the absolute difference between each value's count in the two arrays; every cross-array swap that helps resolves two units of imbalance for one value and, by freely re-pairing beforehand, simultaneously resolves two units for another -- so the minimum number of paid swaps is that total imbalance divided by four.

edge_inputs.description: already identical up to a free within-array reordering
one paid swap needed, then a free reorder finishes it
disjoint value sets, impossible to equalize
minimum size arrays, exactly two elements each
combined counts all even but no single position pairs up the needed values directly -- still solvable in one paid swap because within-array reordering happens before the cross swap
already-equal multisets require zero paid swaps even though the arrays are not identical position-by-position
a single value has an odd combined total buried among otherwise-balanced values
every value already balanced 1-1 between the two arrays, zero cost despite different order

stress.description: random large arrays across the constraint range, imbalance spread across many values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 80000))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 80000) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 80000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1, nums2]

stress.description: small value range so combined counts are frequently even and equalization is often possible
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 20000))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(1, 25) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(1, 25) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1, nums2]

oracle_python:
from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def minCost(self, nums1, nums2):
        # independent construction: enumerate every distinct value directly off the union of
        # keys and accumulate feasibility/imbalance in a single pass, instead of computing two
        # standalone Counters and a set-comprehension sum in one expression.
        c1 = Counter(nums1)
        c2 = Counter(nums2)
        all_values = set(c1.keys())
        all_values.update(c2.keys())
        imbalance = 0
        for v in all_values:
            a = c1[v]
            b = c2[v]
            if (a + b) % 2 == 1:
                return -1
            imbalance += abs(a - b)
        return imbalance // 4
